metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete grep-based proving command for each content AC (e.g., `grep -w 'reconcile' docs/architecture.md`, `grep -l 'Two writers' docs/architecture.md`, and per-file cross-ref checks) so that CI or a reviewer command can fail if required content is absent.
findings.finding: ACs 1–8 (content presence and correctness in docs/architecture.md and three docstrings) have no concrete proving commands — `make lint` and `make typecheck` only verify Python syntax/types, not whether the required section text, vocabulary list, or cross-refs are actually present. An agent could write a stub section or omit a vocabulary term and still pass all listed checks.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (ACs 1–8) and Verification section (make lint / make typecheck only)
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a verification block to the ACs such as:
```
# Verify section exists
grep -c '## Two writers, one store' docs/architecture.md  # expect 1
# Verify vocabulary list
grep -E 'reconcile.*apply.*batch.*sync|reconcile|apply|batch|sync' docs/architecture.md
# Verify false-friend callouts
grep '_apply_batch' docs/architecture.md
grep '_write_event_file' docs/architecture.md
# Verify docstring cross-refs
grep -l 'Two writers' src/rebar/_store/event_append.py src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py
```
These commands can be run by the executor and by a reviewer to confirm done-ness.

findings.finding: Destructive/irreversible operation(s) with no stated safeguard.
findings.suggested_fix: State the safeguard (backup, dry-run, reversible migration, rollback) or justify the irreversibility explicitly (T4).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a signpost docstring in `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py` pointing to the 'Two writers, one store' section in `docs/architecture.md`, OR add a Note explaining why its existing detailed docstring (lines 1–30) makes a signpost redundant — matching the explicit rationale already given for `__main__.py`.
findings.finding: The plan's Scope explicitly names `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py` as part of the local write path ('inline cores in `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py`'), but the plan adds no docstring signpost there, and gives no rationale for the exclusion. Unlike `__main__.py` (which is explicitly dropped with a stated reason), `txn.py` is a prominent grep target for 'commit' — it does the inline `git add`+`git commit` under the lock — and an agent landing there sees no pointer to the 'Two writers, one store' section.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line cross-ref to txn.py's module docstring after the existing byte-parity paragraph: `See docs/architecture.md §"Two writers, one store" for the full local-write-path vs. reconciler-client distinction.`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or a note in the Scope section) specifying that the 'Two writers, one store' section must note that `txn.py` holds its own inline git-add+commit inside the lock and does NOT go through `event_append.stage_and_commit`, to prevent conflating the two local write paths.
findings.finding: The plan's Scope section names `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py` as an 'inline core' of the local store write path alongside `event_append.py`, but no AC requires the architecture.md section to note that txn.py deliberately does NOT go through `stage_and_commit`/`write_and_push`. txn.py's own module docstring (lines 22–23) explicitly calls this out: 'This still does NOT use rebar._store.event_append.stage_and_commit/write_and_push (which re-acquire the lock per event).' Without this clarification, the new docs section could create a new misconception that txn.py and event_append.py share the same commit path.
findings.location: Scope section ('inline cores in src/rebar/_commands/txn.py') and Acceptance Criteria (no AC covers txn.py's divergence from stage_and_commit)
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '[ ] The section notes that `rebar._commands.txn` holds its own inline `git add`+`commit` inside the lock and does not delegate to `event_append.stage_and_commit` (which re-acquires the lock per event) — the two share only the canonical serializer and `event_filename`.' Alternatively, add this distinction as a bullet under the 'Two writers' local-path description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the intended placement of the new section in `docs/architecture.md` (e.g., 'append after the existing Storage section' or 'insert after the ## Reconciler section') so the executor has a concrete anchor.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify where in `docs/architecture.md` the 'Two writers, one store' section should be inserted (e.g., after which existing section, at the end, etc.), leaving placement to executor discretion.
findings.location: Scope bullet 1: 'Add a "Two writers, one store" section to `docs/architecture.md`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope bullet: '…appended after the existing `## Store` (or equivalent) section in `docs/architecture.md`.' If no obvious anchor section exists, state 'appended at the end of the file.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
